tickets.title: Introduce 'wp_ai_client_cache_group' filter to allow customization of the object cache group
tickets.summary: Adds the `wp_ai_client_cache_group` filter to `WP_AI_Client_Cache`, allowing plugins, hosts, and other integrators to replace the default `wp_ai_client` object-cache group. All single and multiple cache operations, deletion, existence checks, and group flushing now use the filtered group. The default behavior is unchanged when the filter is not used, and non-string filter values are cast to strings.
tickets.documentation_rationale: This is a new public filter in the AI Client and is relevant to plugin developers, hosting providers, multisite integrators, and persistent object-cache implementations. It needs documentation and an example, but it is too small for a standalone Dev Note and should be grouped into a miscellaneous AI Client Dev Note.
developer_impact: The filter applies to all operations performed by `WP_AI_Client_Cache`.
Filter return values are cast to string.
tickets.backward_compatibility: Backward compatible. Without the filter, the existing `wp_ai_client` cache group remains in use.
reviewed_sources: Full Trac ticket description and history
tickets.review_notes: The custom query and committed changeset show this ticket as fixed for 7.1. One fetched rendering of the ticket header showed stale 'new/Awaiting Review' metadata, but the linked PR closure and r62557 explicitly record that the ticket was fixed.

tickets.title: AI Client: generate_result() missing test coverage when wp_supports_ai() returns false
tickets.summary: Adds PHPUnit coverage confirming that `WP_AI_Client_Prompt_Builder::generate_result()` returns a `WP_Error` with the existing `prompt_prevented` code and the existing AI-disabled message when `wp_supports_ai()` returns false.
tickets.documentation_rationale: The committed change adds test coverage only. It does not introduce or change a public API, runtime behavior, error code, error message, or compatibility requirement, so it does not need a Dev Note or Field Guide mention.
developer_impact: No public API or behavior change.
Existing AI-disabled error behavior is now covered by a regression test.
reviewed_sources: Full Trac ticket description and history
